author: Yifan Zhou et al.

readme.entry: - **[Ideas Have Genomes: Benchmarking Scientific Lineage Reasoning and Lineage-Grounded Idea Generation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08758)** — Yifan Zhou et al. — <https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08758> · *paper/benchmark/code* — IdeaGene-Bench evaluates AI scientists with 42 lineage-reasoning task types and a lineage-conditioned generation arena rather than treating scientific ideas as isolated outputs. 🆕

factuality.reason: structured evidence plus fetched source snapshot

quality.reason: concrete eval artifacts or methodology signals present

recency.reason: evidence indicates 2025-2026 or actively updated source

section: 9 · Agent-specific evaluation (trajectories, tool use, multi-turn, world state, multi-agent, localization)
source_seam: Hugging Face Daily Papers and alphaXiv hot-paper discovery
title: Ideas Have Genomes: Benchmarking Scientific Lineage Reasoning and Lineage-Grounded Idea Generation

worker.candidate_reason: Adds a concrete evaluation surface for AI-scientist agents: evidence-grounded lineage objects, exact-match reasoning tasks, a structured generation score, released evaluators, and results across 14 LLM-based scientist systems expose a clear compositional bottleneck.
worker.evidence: Grounded in the direct arXiv abstract plus the public VisionXLab/IdeasHaveGenomes repository; alphaXiv was orientation only. The release documents 1,961 golden lineage traces, 1,085 Idea Genome objects, 920 GenomeDiff records across 10 domains, IG-Exam with 42 task types and 1,029 instances, runnable exact-match and PES evaluators, and a strongest-system exact accuracy of 27.3%.
worker.summary: IdeaGene-Bench evaluates AI scientists with 42 lineage-reasoning task types and a lineage-conditioned generation arena rather than treating scientific ideas as isolated outputs.
